title: Quiz: Classical Molecular Dynamics
description: Test your understanding of molecular dynamics simulation fundamentals

questions.question: What fundamental equation is solved in molecular dynamics?
questions.explanation: Molecular dynamics solves Newton's equations of motion, F = ma, for all atoms in the system. This gives trajectories of positions and velocities over time.

questions.question: What is the typical time step used in MD simulations?
questions.explanation: A typical MD time step is 1-2 femtoseconds (10⁻¹⁵ s), which is about 10-20× smaller than the fastest molecular vibrations (bond stretching at ~10-30 fs period).

questions.question: Which force field term describes bond stretching?
questions.explanation: Bond stretching is typically modeled as a harmonic potential V = ½k(r - r₀)², where k is the force constant and r₀ is the equilibrium bond length.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a thermostat in MD?

options.text: To maintain constant temperature by coupling to a heat bath

questions.explanation: A thermostat maintains constant temperature by coupling the system to a heat bath. This allows sampling of the canonical (NVT) ensemble instead of the microcanonical (NVE).

questions.question: In an NVE ensemble, the total energy (kinetic + potential) is conserved.
questions.explanation: True. The NVE (microcanonical) ensemble has constant Number of particles, Volume, and Energy. In the absence of thermostats, MD naturally conserves total energy.

questions.question: What is the purpose of periodic boundary conditions?

options.text: To eliminate surface effects and simulate bulk properties

questions.explanation: Periodic boundary conditions (PBC) replicate the simulation box infinitely, eliminating surfaces. This allows simulation of bulk behavior with a finite number of particles.

questions.question: What property can be calculated from the mean square displacement (MSD)?
questions.explanation: The diffusion coefficient D is calculated from MSD: D = lim(t→∞) MSD(t)/(6t) in 3D. This Einstein relation connects microscopic motion to macroscopic transport.

questions.question: Which are properties of the Velocity Verlet algorithm? (Select all)

options.text: Requires only one force evaluation per step

questions.explanation: Velocity Verlet is time-reversible, symplectic, and requires only one force evaluation per step. While it conserves energy well, it doesn't conserve it exactly (small oscillations due to discretization).
